title: What is the difference between Standard and Premium Dedicated Relays?
subtitle: Comparing relay performance, latency, and features.
content:
• Standard Nodes: Shared high-speed relays suitable for routine automated backup, remote database queries, and lightweight cross-border collaboration.
  • Premium Dedicated Relays: Deployed over multi-ingress BGP premium transit backbones (optimized for regions like Hong Kong, Singapore, and Japan). These nodes feature dedicated bandwidth allocations, zero packet loss under peak-hour congestion, and ultra-low jitter, tailor-made for high-throughput enterprise operations and low-latency virtual workspaces.

title: What are the concurrent device limitations for a single account?
subtitle: Limits on simultaneous device connections.
content: To prevent commercial line abuse and maintain consistent bandwidth availability for all premium consumers, a standard individual premium license permits up to 3 concurrent active device connections simultaneously. Sharing configuration strings publicly will trigger automated security rate-limiting on our billing gateway.

title: What are the pricing tiers for premium membership plans?
subtitle: Discover the ideal premium secure subscription package for your needs.
content: OVOSEC Premium plans are structured on monthly, semi-annual, and annual terms. Longer subscription commitments feature discounted rates. You can view all pricing details and active seasonal specials on our official Home Page plan grid.

title: How do I upgrade my account status to premium?
subtitle: Unlocking all high-speed nodes and enjoying unlimited priority bandwidth.
content: To upgrade, access your account through our Login Page, navigate to the billing section, select your preferred membership tier, and complete payment using a credit card or local banking gateway.

title: Why do my Secure Relay Endpoints show "Timeout" or "-1ms" during the connection test?
subtitle: Understanding ping timeout indicators.
content:
A "Timeout" status often indicates that your local network provider or corporate firewall is blocking ICMP/Ping requests (the test packets), but it does not mean the data path is broken.

Please try connecting to the node regardless. If data flows normally after clicking "Connect", you can safely ignore the timeout indicator.

title: How can I optimize the connection for low-latency online gaming and voice chat?
subtitle: Optimizing settings for multiplayer gaming and VoIP apps.
content:
For multiplayer gaming or real-time VoIP apps, UDP packet transmission is vital. To optimize your experience, please follow these steps:

  • Ensure you are connected to our Premium Dedicated Relays (marked with BGP or Optimized tags).
  • In the app settings, navigate to Protocol Settings / Core Settings and ensure "UDP Forwarding" or "Enable UDP" is toggled ON. This dramatically reduces packet loss and multi-player jitter.
